summary: Web application with a feedback system that requires admin authentication (username/password) to approve feedback, which is then displayed in the feedback section of the main website.
architectural_notes: Feedback approval is gated behind admin authentication (username/password)
Only approved feedback is shown in the public-facing feedback section

existing_features.title: In the AdminDashboard, ensure the admin login form (AdminLoginForm) uses the stored username and password credentials for authentication. After successful login, the feedback management tab must list all submitted feedback and allow the authenticated admin to approve or reject each item using inline approve/reject actions.

In the ReviewsSection on the homepage, fetch only feedback items with an 'approved' status from the backend and display them alongside the hardcoded seed reviews. Non-approved (pending or rejected) feedback must not appear in this section.

new_feature_requests.summary: Admin login with username/password to approve submitted feedback
Display approved feedback in the feedback section of the main website

Ensure the backend stores a hashed or plaintext admin username and password, and the adminLogin function validates credentials and returns an admin token or session that grants permission to approve or reject feedback. The approved/rejected status field on feedback records must be persisted in stable storage.
